title: Orchestrate credential profiles and ergonomic pullstream bridging
preview: Context: We've had ongoing divergence across the Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs for credential fallbacks, retry presets, and the pullstream (streaming) ergonomics. Teams reported brittle reauth interactions during long-lived streams and inconsistent retry defaults across SDKs leading to different production behavior. Thi...
text:
Context: We've had ongoing divergence across the Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs for credential fallbacks, retry presets, and the pullstream (streaming) ergonomics. Teams reported brittle reauth interactions during long-lived streams and inconsistent retry defaults across SDKs leading to different production behavior. This PR centralizes the credential profile definitions, introduces an ergonomic PullStreamBridge to harmonize consumer-facing streaming patterns, and bumps tri-SDK versions to align expectations.

Motivation: 1) Make credential fallback behavior predictable across languages (delegated, cascade, and local-cache modes). 2) Provide clear retry presets (latency-sensitive, throughput-optimized, and conservative) that map to runtime policies. 3) Offer a small, opt-in PullStreamBridge API that simplifies checkpointing/resume, peek semantics, and a cancelable iterator pattern that mat...

title: Graceful disconnect: buffered tool-call retry and reconnect window for streaming API
preview: Motivation: We observed customer incidents where short-lived client disconnects during multi-step function/tool-calling flows caused duplicate tool invocations or orphaned streaming sessions, leading to higher costs and inconsistent structured outputs. This PR introduces a server-side in-flight buffer and a short re...
text: Motivation: We observed customer incidents where short-lived client disconnects during multi-step function/tool-calling flows caused duplicate tool invocations or orphaned streaming sessions, leading to higher costs and inconsistent structured outputs. This PR introduces a server-side in-flight buffer and a short reconnect window with idempotency reconciliation to preserve end-to-end semantics while keeping latency and cost in check. Summary of changes: 1) Session manager: add a buffered-inflight queue that retains in-progress tool invocations for a configurable reconnect window (default 20s). Sessions now emit a compact session token (reconnect_token) on streaming start. 2) Tool invocation idempotency: introduce X-Tool-Invocation-Id header and runtime-side idempotency table so duplicate replays are deduplicated; tools should return idempotency-safe responses. 3) Reconnect/reconcile f...

title: implement signal smoothing and traffic-granularity guard for noisy tail regressions
preview: Motivation: Recent canary runs showed recurrent false-positive tail-latency alerts during short traffic spikes, and we observed noisy variance for small-batch inference workloads. This PR introduces a two-part defense: 1) a lightweight temporal signal smoother that reduces high-frequency noise in per-route latency f...
text:
Motivation: Recent canary runs showed recurrent false-positive tail-latency alerts during short traffic spikes, and we observed noisy variance for small-batch inference workloads. This PR introduces a two-part defense: 1) a lightweight temporal signal smoother that reduces high-frequency noise in per-route latency fingerprints while preserving changepoints, and 2) a traffic-granularity guard that slices canary evaluations by traffic orthogonals (route, payload-size bucket, and client tier) and gates rollouts when small, concentrated cohorts drive apparent regressions.

Goals: reduce alert churn, avoid unnecessary rollbacks for bursty micro-cohorts, and improve detection power for sustained regressions. This is intended as a conservative enhancement — default behavior is opt-in per-alert and the rollout guard has configurable thresholds and cooldown periods.

What changed (high-level):...

title: Consensus-backed metadata rollup and reader-friendly changelog
preview: Context and motivation: We onboard models from a variety of sources (author-uploaded manifests, CI-provided metadata, and discovery probes). Discrepancies between sources (missing fields, differing perf metrics, variant aliases) have caused noisy changelogs and occasional release regressions. This PR implements a de...
text:
Context and motivation: We onboard models from a variety of sources (author-uploaded manifests, CI-provided metadata, and discovery probes). Discrepancies between sources (missing fields, differing perf metrics, variant aliases) have caused noisy changelogs and occasional release regressions. This PR implements a deterministic "consensus rollup" step during intake that: 1) canonicalizes manifest keys and field formats; 2) merges performance profile vectors using a configurable aggregator (weighted median by signal confidence); 3) records provenance for each field so downstream systems can explain where a value came from; and 4) emits a concise, reader-friendly changelog "teaser" that groups changes into: metadata edits, perf deltas, and variant-level lifecycle events.

What changed (high level):
- New consensus rollup service (registry/rollup) that takes a set of manifests and returns...

title: Passive prefill operator and econ blueprint for safe defaults
preview: Motivation: Many customers want lower per-token unit economics without increasing tail latency. Existing aggressive prefill strategies improved cost but occasionally increased 95/99p latency during cold tails or introduced cache pollution. This PR introduces a passive prefill operator (runtime) plus an 'econ operato...
text:
Motivation: Many customers want lower per-token unit economics without increasing tail latency. Existing aggressive prefill strategies improved cost but occasionally increased 95/99p latency during cold tails or introduced cache pollution. This PR introduces a passive prefill operator (runtime) plus an 'econ operator' blueprint (console + defaults) to provide safe, opt-in cost defaults that preserve latency SLOs.

What this PR does: implements a passive prefill path that populates KV/prefix caches in the background without blocking client responses, adds an econ operator that enforces budget windows and caps (max-prefill-tokens, adaptive-mini-batch limits), and ships a console blueprint with vetted defaults for common workloads (chat, single-turn generation, embeddings). The blueprint exposes: safe batch/timeout profiles, quantization profile recommendations, and a fallback policy tha...

title: prevent-downgrades-and-standardize-on-disk-backup-format
preview: Motivation: Past upgrades have exposed two recurring problems for private deployments: accidental downgrades (operator error or rollback scripts that do not account for schema/feature flags) and inconsistent backup formats across installer versions which makes restores brittle. This PR introduces a lightweight downg...
text:
Motivation: Past upgrades have exposed two recurring problems for private deployments: accidental downgrades (operator error or rollback scripts that do not account for schema/feature flags) and inconsistent backup formats across installer versions which makes restores brittle. This PR introduces a lightweight downgrade-blocker in the installer, pins on-disk backup metadata to a stable v2 format, and adds a deterministic restore test to the CI matrix.

Summary of changes: Added a check in the private installer that refuses to apply a release with an older semantic version if that release would require a schema downgrade; the check is driven by a new release-metadata.json (version + schema_version + migration_path). Implemented a stable on-disk backup v2 format (single tar.gz with manifests/etcd-snapshots/secrets.enc and a compact manifest.json) and utilities to produce/consume it. Int...

title: Sampled probe choreographer and on-call handoff scorecard
preview: Motivation: Reduce noisy probes and pager fatigue while improving first-responder context during handoffs by sampling probes intelligently and producing a compact handoff scorecard. This PR implements a sampled probe choreographer that schedules a small, representative set of lightweight probes per on-call window, t...
text: Motivation: Reduce noisy probes and pager fatigue while improving first-responder context during handoffs by sampling probes intelligently and producing a compact handoff scorecard. This PR implements a sampled probe choreographer that schedules a small, representative set of lightweight probes per on-call window, tracks probe provenance, and emits a one-line handoff scorecard summarizing signal health and probe coverage. Changes include: 1) a new choreographer component (choreographer/sampled_scheduler.py) that picks probes deterministically using a weighted reservoir sampler tied to on-call rotation ids; 2) probe metadata propagation to the incident snapshot (snapshot/attachments.md -> snapshot metadata additions) so responders can see sample reason and TTL; 3) a handoff scorecard generator (handoff/scorecard.py) that produces a compact human-friendly summary and machine-readable ta...

title: introduce-rag-microservice-tool-calling-docker-quickstart
preview: Add a compact, docker-first RAG microservice quickstart that demonstrates function/tool-calling, streaming responses, and a minimal KV cache for low-latency private deployments. Motivation: customers frequently ask for a small, deployable reference that shows how to wire Redwood SDK into a tiny service that handles ...
text: Add a compact, docker-first RAG microservice quickstart that demonstrates function/tool-calling, streaming responses, and a minimal KV cache for low-latency private deployments. Motivation: customers frequently ask for a small, deployable reference that shows how to wire Redwood SDK into a tiny service that handles document retrieval, model orchestration, and calls external tools (search + knowledge connector) with clear observability hooks. This PR delivers: 1) examples/rag-microservice/ - a ready-to-run template (Dockerfile + docker-compose) that boots a single-node instance with an embedded retriever, Redwood SDK integration, and a simple tool interface for invoking a mock search API. 2) README quickstart that walks through local Docker run, env var configuration for hosted vs. private endpoints, and sample curl/SDK calls for streaming. 3) src/agent.ts - an opinionated agent orches...

title: capacity-leasebook-and-throttle-harmonizer-for-stable-dedicated-throughput
preview: Context: Dedicated customers observed P95 latency spikes during short high-frequency bursts that consumed warm-pool headroom and caused aggressive preemption of committed capacity; this PR introduces a Capacity Leasebook and a Throttle Harmonizer to smooth those microbursts and preserve long-running throughput guara...
text: Context: Dedicated customers observed P95 latency spikes during short high-frequency bursts that consumed warm-pool headroom and caused aggressive preemption of committed capacity; this PR introduces a Capacity Leasebook and a Throttle Harmonizer to smooth those microbursts and preserve long-running throughput guarantees. Motivation: decouple short-lived burst reservations from sustained commitments so that priority tenants see consistent throughput while still allowing opportunistic short bursts. High-level changes: new leasebook controller that issues timeboxed 'leases' for burst capacity; soft-lease arbitration algorithm (probabilistic decay + runway smoothing) to avoid synchronized drain; harmonizer in scheduler that computes gentle per-tenant throttles when short-lease utilization exceeds configured runway thresholds; telemetry for per-lease lifespan histograms, preemption counts...

title: Orchestrated A/B-Test Anchoring with Fast-Fallback and promotion hooks
preview: Motivation: Teams running A/B tests and canary promotions need deterministic cohort anchoring plus an automated fast-fallback when latency, error-rate, or quality regressions are detected. This PR introduces a controller and API surface that: 1) pins user/query cohorts to a stable treatment (anchor) during long-runn...
text:
Motivation: Teams running A/B tests and canary promotions need deterministic cohort anchoring plus an automated fast-fallback when latency, error-rate, or quality regressions are detected. This PR introduces a controller and API surface that: 1) pins user/query cohorts to a stable treatment (anchor) during long-running A/B experiments, 2) provides promotion hooks to move anchored cohorts from shadow->canary->primary on a schedule or metric gate, and 3) implements a fast-fallback path that immediately drains traffic from a failing variant and re-anchors affected cohorts to the last known-good variant.

Changes include: controller implementation (orchestration/scheduler), a new lightweight cohort store (kv-backed, optional in Dedicated/Private), API surface for experiments (create/update/pause/promote), telemetry and probe instrumentation (per-cohort latency/error/quality counters), and...

title: introduce-tranche-econ-tiler-prefill-optimizer-and-cli
preview: Motivation: Tenant workloads show multimodal sequence-length and arrival distributions; static batch/caching defaults lead to suboptimal unit economics. This PR introduces the Tranche Econ Tiler (TET) — a runtime component plus a small CLI to infer tenant-prefill "tranches" (batch shape + KV-cache allocation + quant...
text:
Motivation: Tenant workloads show multimodal sequence-length and arrival distributions; static batch/caching defaults lead to suboptimal unit economics. This PR introduces the Tranche Econ Tiler (TET) — a runtime component plus a small CLI to infer tenant-prefill "tranches" (batch shape + KV-cache allocation + quant tier) from historical traffic and cost signals, and emit safe defaults for the autoscaler and cost-suggester.

What this changes: Adds a new runtime component (tranche-tiler) that runs as part of the prefill pipeline. TET: (1) ingests sample windows, (2) computes candidate tranche templates using a tiling strategy that balances latency SLOs vs tokens/unit cost, (3) scores candidates with the unit-econ evaluator, and (4) exposes an HTTP endpoint and a CLI to export curated defaults as YAML presets. The CLI supports dry-run mode and a deterministic seed to reproduce tranche ...

title: add-invocation-telemetry-deduper-and-token-collapse-visualizer
preview: Introduce a lightweight Telemetry Deduper service and a Token Collapse Visualizer dashboard to reduce noisy duplicate spans and surface redundant token-level cost patterns. Motivation: during several incidents and routine SLO tuning we observed high cardinality and alert fatigue caused by duplicated spans emitted fr...
text: Introduce a lightweight Telemetry Deduper service and a Token Collapse Visualizer dashboard to reduce noisy duplicate spans and surface redundant token-level cost patterns. Motivation: during several incidents and routine SLO tuning we observed high cardinality and alert fatigue caused by duplicated spans emitted from the serving runtime (frontend retries, duplicate instrumentation points, and batched replays). This PR implements a deterministic fingerprinting pipeline that collapses semantically-identical spans early in the observability ingestion path and a dashboard that helps engineers understand when token costs are dominated by collapsed/duplicate traffic. The change includes a TTL-backed fingerprint cache, session-aware collapse heuristics, an orthogonal latency decomposition helper ("orthogonalizer") that attributes latency to queue/io/model phases for collapsed invocations, a...

title: Add telemetry events + dashboard hooks for marketplace onboarding funnel
preview: Context: As part of the Cloud Marketplace Listing Refresh, we need reliable, provider-consistent instrumentation to measure the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key/usage funnel and identify drop-offs. Today we have partial logs and a few ad-hoc metrics, but no stable analytics taxonomy and ...
text:
Context: As part of the Cloud Marketplace Listing Refresh, we need reliable, provider-consistent instrumentation to measure the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key/usage funnel and identify drop-offs. Today we have partial logs and a few ad-hoc metrics, but no stable analytics taxonomy and no standard error/latency dimensions to support Partnerships + Growth reporting. This PR introduces a small set of canonical marketplace onboarding telemetry events emitted by the console + control-plane API, plus a metrics bridge so we can build dashboards/alerts without scraping logs.

Summary of changes: (1) Adds canonical analytics events for marketplace onboarding across AWS/GCP flows. (2) Adds latency + error code dimensions for entitlement verification and org linking steps. (3) Adds a lightweight server-side aggregator that emits Prometheus counters/histograms to su...
links: Linear: ENG-22114 — Add telemetry for marketplace onboarding funnel
Jira: SUP-4821 — Marketplace subscription provisioning delay (instrumentation added to measure waiting state prevalence)

title: Add AWS/GCP Marketplace onboarding pages + entitlement troubleshooting
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-class AWS and GCP Marketplace onboarding docs to reduce time-to-first-token for marketplace buyers and to unblock partner submission requirements. It introduces a Marketplace onboarding landing page, provider-specific step-by-step guides (Subscribe → Entitlement → Provision org → Create A...
text:
Summary: This PR adds first-class AWS and GCP Marketplace onboarding docs to reduce time-to-first-token for marketplace buyers and to unblock partner submission requirements. It introduces a Marketplace onboarding landing page, provider-specific step-by-step guides (Subscribe → Entitlement → Provision org → Create API key → First request), and an entitlement troubleshooting page with concrete error states and recommended next actions. It also links to the Marketplace Security Documentation Pack and clarifies which security artifacts are public vs available on request.

Motivation / context: Marketplace buyers are procurement-led and frequently get stuck during entitlement propagation and org linking. Partner feedback requested clearer customer-facing steps and explicit guidance for common failure modes (e.g., existing orgs, multi-subscription, delayed entitlement sync). This doc set i...

title: Add minimal chat app template to accelerate Hosted API activation
preview: This PR adds a small, copy-pasteable "minimal chat app" example intended to reduce time-to-first-token for new Hosted API users. The goal is to provide a single folder that can be run in <5 minutes with an API key, supports streaming by default, and matches the onboarding email + console nudge CTAs ("Make your first...
text:
This PR adds a small, copy-pasteable "minimal chat app" example intended to reduce time-to-first-token for new Hosted API users. The goal is to provide a single folder that can be run in <5 minutes with an API key, supports streaming by default, and matches the onboarding email + console nudge CTAs ("Make your first request" / "Enable streaming"). It also includes lightweight telemetry guidance so the Growth funnel can attribute activation accelerators without collecting sensitive payloads.

What’s included: (1) New example at examples/minimal-chat-app/ implemented as a Node + Express server with a tiny static UI. Server proxies to Redwood Hosted API using the Redwood TS SDK, streams tokens to the browser via SSE, and shows basic error states (missing/invalid API key, rate limited). (2) README updates: top-level README now highlights the minimal chat app as the recommended first proje...

title: Add “Hello world” first request snippet aligned with activation CTAs
preview:
## Context
This sprint’s primary activation KPI is “first successful request” and “first 100k tokens” within 7 days. We’ve seen drop-off at the earliest step due to (a) too many options in the quickstart, (b) mismatch between lifecycle email CTAs and what the docs show, and (c) missing/unclear env var and model defa...
text:
## Context
This sprint’s primary activation KPI is “first successful request” and “first 100k tokens” within 7 days. We’ve seen drop-off at the earliest step due to (a) too many options in the quickstart, (b) mismatch between lifecycle email CTAs and what the docs show, and (c) missing/unclear env var and model defaults.

This PR adds a minimal, copy/paste “Hello world” snippet that matches the onboarding email v2 + console onboarding checklist CTA wording: “Make your first request.” The snippet is intentionally small: create API key → export env var → single streaming request.

## What changed
- New `hello-world` page that is optimized for TTFT (goal: <2 minutes from API key creation to first streamed token).
- Adds 3 variants with identical structure:
 - curl (streaming)
 - Node/TypeScript (SDK)
 - Python (SDK)
- Standardizes naming used across growth surfaces:
 - Env var: `REDWOOD_...

title: Reduce CPU syscalls and thread handoff on request path
preview: Motivation: CPU-side bottlenecks on the request hot path were dominated by frequent syscalls and expensive thread handoffs when requests crossed the network/IO boundary. This PR reduces syscall frequency and simplifies handoff between acceptor/dispatcher threads and worker pools to lower tail latency and overall CPU...
text: Motivation: CPU-side bottlenecks on the request hot path were dominated by frequent syscalls and expensive thread handoffs when requests crossed the network/IO boundary. This PR reduces syscall frequency and simplifies handoff between acceptor/dispatcher threads and worker pools to lower tail latency and overall CPU usage. Summary of approach: 1) Replace a large fraction of blocking read()/write()/select() syscalls on the hot path with an edge-triggered epoll-based poll loop + per-core submission queues to amortize kernel transitions; 2) Introduce a light-weight per-core MPMC ring buffer (lock-free where supported) used to hand off ready requests to CPU-local worker shards instead of parking/unparking threads; 3) Move request serialization (marshalling) off the acceptor path onto a short-lived serialization worker that batches small requests together (continuous batching window = 1.5m...

title: Add analytics events for benchmark page interactions + conversions
preview:
Context
We need consistent, queryable analytics for the public benchmark landing page + interactive table so Marketing Ops can report on benchmark launch KPIs (engagement → signup/demo) and Sales can see top-of-funnel intent signals. This PR adds client-side instrumentation for the key interactions called out in PM-...
text:
Context
We need consistent, queryable analytics for the public benchmark landing page + interactive table so Marketing Ops can report on benchmark launch KPIs (engagement → signup/demo) and Sales can see top-of-funnel intent signals. This PR adds client-side instrumentation for the key interactions called out in PM-826.

What this PR does
- Adds a small benchmark-specific event schema (packages/analytics/src/events/benchmark.ts) and emits events from the benchmark page UI.
- Emits events for:
 - Filter changes (model, region, context length bucket)
 - Metric toggle changes (latency/$ vs p95 latency vs $/1M tokens)
 - Export clicks (CSV, JSON)
 - CTA clicks (Start free trial, Request demo, Dedicated sizing)
- Normalizes payload fields so downstream dashboards don’t require bespoke parsing.
- Includes basic payload validation + unit tests to prevent accidental breaking changes.

Event n...

title: Add feature flag + cohorting rules for Console onboarding checklist (with kill switch)
preview: Context: We’re rolling out the new onboarding / “first production launch” checklist in Console behind a feature flag, with progressive exposure by cohort. This PR introduces the first-cut flag definition, deterministic cohort assignment, and an emergency kill-switch path that Support/SRE can use if we see regression...
text: Context: We’re rolling out the new onboarding / “first production launch” checklist in Console behind a feature flag, with progressive exposure by cohort. This PR introduces the first-cut flag definition, deterministic cohort assignment, and an emergency kill-switch path that Support/SRE can use if we see regressions during dogfood or cohort rollout. Summary of changes: (1) Adds flag definition `console.onboarding_checklist` with structured config: `enabled`, `mode`, `allowlist_tenants`, `denylist_tenants`, `rollout_percentage`, `cohort_seed`, and `kill_switch` (server-side hard disable). (2) Implements deterministic cohort assignment using stable hashing on `tenant_id` + `cohort_seed` to avoid cohort “flapping” between requests and across regions. (3) Adds a small policy layer to compute exposure in the control plane so Console can request “shouldShowChecklist” with a single API call...

title: router: add FPE decision tracing + enforce pinned-build fallback guardrails
tenant-buffer-pool-rebalancer-and-utilization-signal-mediator
preview: Motivation: tenants with bursty traffic were observed to induce oscillatory reassignments of reserved buffer credits during contention windows which degraded P99 latency for mid-tier customers. This PR introduces a conservative, proportional rebalancer for tenant buffer pools combined with a utilization-signal media...
text: Motivation: tenants with bursty traffic were observed to induce oscillatory reassignments of reserved buffer credits during contention windows which degraded P99 latency for mid-tier customers. This PR introduces a conservative, proportional rebalancer for tenant buffer pools combined with a utilization-signal mediation layer that smooths short-term noise and applies a fairness gate to protect latency-sensitive SLOs. The change is opt-in behind a feature flag and includes backward-compatible fallbacks to the existing allocation ledger. Implementation summary: 1) controller: tenant_buffer_rebalancer - samples per-tenant occupancy and in-flight rates, computes target deltas using a damped proportional allocator, and emits reassignments via the existing reservation API; 2) util-signal-mediator: lightweight EWMA smoothing with configurable half-life and an outlier clamp that prevents a si...
